compiled_state.title: ticket CLI: -p shorthand maps to --parent not --priority -> agent priority assignment silently sets wrong field
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: The ticket create CLI uses -p as a shorthand for --parent (parent ticket ID). However, agents naturally interpret -p as --priority (matching common CLI conventions). When an agent attempts 'ticket create epic ... -p 2', it silently sets parent=2 instead of priority=2, causing a 'parent ticket does not exist' error that is hard to diagnose. Consider adding -P or --prio as a shorthand for --priority, or making the -p ambiguity more explicit in error messages.
